content.text: I need to build an error handling system for this API.
I'll set up a global error handler. Should I start with the middleware or the error classes?
No, start with the error classes first. We need NotFoundError, ValidationError, and AuthError.
Thanks. Can you also add input validation for the user registration endpoint?
I'll add validation to the registration endpoint. Do you want me to validate the email, password, and username fields?
No, not the password field — we handle password validation on the frontend. Just email and username.
Good. Now I want to add a feature where users can report errors they encounter. The error report should include what they were doing wrong and what they expected to happen instead.

I'll add an error reporting endpoint.

```python
@app.route('/reports', methods=['POST'])
def submit_error_report():
    data = request.get_json()
    
    if not data.get('description'):
        raise ValidationError('Description is required')
    
    report = ErrorReport(
        user_id=get_current_user().id,
        description=data['description'],
        expected_behavior=data.get('expected_behavior'),
        steps_to_reproduce=data.get('steps'),
    )
    db.session.add(report)
    db.session.commit()
    
    return jsonify({'id': report.id, 'status': 'submitted'}), 201
```
In the error report model, add a field for 'what went wrong' — the user should describe what the incorrect behavior was.

I'll add that field:

```python
class ErrorReport(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'))
    description = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    what_went_wrong = db.Column(db.Text)
    expected_behavior = db.Column(db.Text)
    steps_to_reproduce = db.Column(db.Text)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
```
We had a bug last week where the stop_processing flag wasn't being checked, causing duplicate error reports. Make sure the endpoint checks that flag.
Got it. Now add rate limiting to the reports endpoint — users shouldn't be able to spam reports.
